    13→from erk.cli.commands.slot.common import is_placeholder_branch
    14→from erk.cli.constants import (
    15→    DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_METADATA_NAME,
    16→    DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME,
    17→    ERK_PLAN_LABEL,
    18→)
    19→from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context
    20→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure, UserFacingCliError
    21→from erk.core.context import ErkContext
    22→from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext
    23→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PullRebaseError, PushError
    24→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import (
    25→    create_submission_queued_block,
    26→    render_erk_issue_event,
    27→)
    28→from erk_shared.gateway.github.parsing import (
    29→    construct_pr_url,
    30→    construct_workflow_run_url,
    31→    extract_owner_repo_from_github_url,
    32→)
    33→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRNotFound
    34→from erk_shared.impl_context import (
    35→    create_impl_context,
    36→    impl_context_exists,
    37→    remove_impl_context,
    38→)
    39→from erk_shared.impl_folder import read_plan_ref, resolve_impl_dir
    40→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output
    41→from erk_shared.plan_store.planned_pr_lifecycle import IMPL_CONTEXT_DIR
    42→from erk_shared.plan_store.types import PlanNotFound
    43→
    44→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    45→
    46→
    47→def load_workflow_config(repo_root: Path, workflow_name: str) -> dict[str, str]:
    48→    """Load workflow config from .erk/config.toml [workflows.<name>] section.
    49→
    50→    Args:
    51→        repo_root: Repository root path
    52→        workflow_name: Workflow filename (with or without .yml/.yaml extension).
    53→            Only the basename is used for config lookup.
    54→
    55→    Returns:
    56→        Dict of string key-value pairs for workflow inputs.
    57→        Returns empty dict if config file or section doesn't exist.
    58→
    59→    Example:
    60→        For workflow_name="plan-implement.yml", reads from:
    61→        .erk/config.toml -> [workflows.plan-implement] section
    62→    """
    63→    config_path = repo_root / ".erk" / "config.toml"
    64→
    65→    if not config_path.exists():
    66→        return {}
    67→
    68→    with open(config_path, "rb") as f:
    69→        data = tomllib.load(f)
    70→
    71→    # Extract basename and strip .yml/.yaml extension
    72→    basename = Path(workflow_name).name
    73→    config_name = basename.removesuffix(".yml").removesuffix(".yaml")
    74→
    75→    # Get [workflows.<name>] section
    76→    workflows_section = data.get("workflows", {})
    77→    workflow_config = workflows_section.get(config_name, {})
    78→
    79→    # Convert all values to strings (workflow inputs are always strings)
    80→    return {k: str(v) for k, v in workflow_config.items()}
    81→
    82→
    83→@dataclass(frozen=True)
    84→class DispatchResult:
    85→    """Result of dispatching a single plan."""
    86→
    87→    plan_number: int
    88→    plan_title: str
    89→    plan_url: str
    90→    pr_number: int | None
    91→    pr_url: str | None
    92→    workflow_run_id: str
    93→    workflow_url: str
    94→
    95→
    96→def _build_workflow_run_url(plan_url: str, run_id: str) -> str:
    97→    """Construct GitHub Actions workflow run URL from plan URL and run ID.
    98→
    99→    Args:
   100→        plan_url: GitHub plan URL (e.g., https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123)
   101→        run_id: Workflow run ID
   102→
   103→    Returns:
   104→        Workflow run URL (e.g., https://github.com/owner/repo/actions/runs/1234567890)
   105→    """
   106→    owner_repo = extract_owner_repo_from_github_url(plan_url)
   107→    if owner_repo is not None:
   108→        owner, repo = owner_repo
   109→        return construct_workflow_run_url(owner, repo, run_id)
   110→    return f"https://github.com/actions/runs/{run_id}"
   111→
   112→
   113→def _build_pr_url(plan_url: str, pr_number: int) -> str:
   114→    """Construct GitHub PR URL from plan URL and PR number.
   115→
   116→    Args:
   117→        plan_url: GitHub plan URL (e.g., https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123)
   118→        pr_number: PR number
   119→
   120→    Returns:
   121→        PR URL (e.g., https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/456)
   122→    """
   123→    owner_repo = extract_owner_repo_from_github_url(plan_url)
   124→    if owner_repo is not None:
   125→        owner, repo = owner_repo
   126→        return construct_pr_url(owner, repo, pr_number)
   127→    return f"https://github.com/pull/{pr_number}"
   128→
   129→
   130→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   131→class ValidatedPlannedPR:
   132→    """Draft PR that passed all validation checks."""
   133→
   134→    number: int
   135→    title: str
   136→    url: str
   137→    branch_name: str
   138→
   139→
   140→def _validate_planned_pr_for_dispatch(
   141→    ctx: ErkContext,
   142→    repo: RepoContext,
   143→    plan_number: int,
   144→) -> ValidatedPlannedPR:
   145→    """Validate a planned PR plan for dispatch.
   146→
   147→    Fetches the PR, validates it has the erk-plan label and is OPEN.
   148→
   149→    Args:
   150→        ctx: ErkContext with git operations
   151→        repo: Repository context
   152→        plan_number: PR number to validate
   153→
   154→    Raises:
   155→        SystemExit: If PR doesn't exist, missing label, or not OPEN.
   156→    """
   157→    pr_result = ctx.github.get_pr(repo.root, plan_number)
   158→    if isinstance(pr_result, PRNotFound):
   159→        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"PR #{plan_number} not found")
   160→        raise SystemExit(1)
   161→
   162→    # Validate: must have erk-plan label
   163→    if ERK_PLAN_LABEL not in pr_result.labels:
   164→        user_output(
   165→            click.style("Error: ", fg="red")
   166→            + f"PR #{plan_number} does not have {ERK_PLAN_LABEL} label\n\n"
   167→            "Cannot dispatch non-plan PRs for automated implementation."
   168→        )
   169→        raise SystemExit(1)
   170→
   171→    # Validate: must be OPEN
   172→    if pr_result.state != "OPEN":
   173→        user_output(
   174→            click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"PR #{plan_number} is {pr_result.state}\n\n"
   175→            "Cannot dispatch closed PRs for automated implementation."
   176→        )
   177→        raise SystemExit(1)
   178→
   179→    return ValidatedPlannedPR(
   180→        number=plan_number,
   181→        title=pr_result.title,
   182→        url=pr_result.url,
   183→        branch_name=pr_result.head_ref_name,
   184→    )
   185→
   186→
   187→def _dispatch_planned_pr_plan(
   188→    ctx: ErkContext,
   189→    *,
   190→    repo: RepoContext,
   191→    validated: ValidatedPlannedPR,
   192→    submitted_by: str,
   193→    original_branch: str,
   194→    base_branch: str,
   195→) -> DispatchResult:
   196→    """Dispatch a validated planned-PR plan for implementation.
   197→
   198→    For planned-PR plans, the branch and PR already exist. This function:
   199→    - Fetches and checks out the existing plan branch
   200→    - Creates .erk/impl-context/ with provider="github-draft-pr"
   201→    - Commits and pushes .erk/impl-context/ to existing branch
   202→    - Triggers the workflow with plan_backend="planned_pr"
   203→
   204→    Args:
   205→        ctx: ErkContext with git operations
   206→        repo: Repository context
   207→        validated: Validated planned PR information
   208→        submitted_by: GitHub username of submitter
   209→        original_branch: Original branch name (to restore after)
   210→        base_branch: Base branch for PR
   211→
   212→    Returns:
   213→        DispatchResult with URLs and identifiers.
   214→    """
   215→    plan_number = validated.number
   216→    branch_name = validated.branch_name
   217→
   218→    # Fetch plan content via PlanBackend
   219→    user_output("Fetching plan content...")
   220→    result = ctx.plan_store.get_plan(repo.root, str(plan_number))
   221→    if isinstance(result, PlanNotFound):
   222→        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"PR #{plan_number}: plan content not found")
   223→        raise SystemExit(1)
   224→    plan = result
   225→
   226→    # Fetch and checkout the existing plan branch
   227→    user_output(f"Checking out existing plan branch: {click.style(branch_name, fg='cyan')}")
   228→    ctx.git.remote.fetch_branch(repo.root, "origin", branch_name)
   229→
   230→    local_branches = ctx.git.branch.list_local_branches(repo.root)
   231→    if branch_name not in local_branches:
   232→        remote_ref = f"origin/{branch_name}"
   233→        ctx.branch_manager.create_tracking_branch(repo.root, branch_name, remote_ref)
   234→
   235→    ctx.branch_manager.checkout_branch(repo.root, branch_name)
   236→
   237→    # Sync local branch with remote (may be behind from prior submission or CI)
   238→    pull_result = ctx.git.remote.pull_rebase(repo.root, "origin", branch_name)
   239→    if isinstance(pull_result, PullRebaseError):
   240→        raise UserFacingCliError(
   241→            f"Failed to sync branch '{branch_name}' with remote: {pull_result.message}"
   242→        )
   243→
   244→    # Clean up previous .erk/impl-context/ if it exists (e.g., from a prior failed dispatch)
   245→    if impl_context_exists(repo.root):
   246→        user_output("Cleaning up previous .erk/impl-context/ folder...")
   247→        remove_impl_context(repo.root)
   248→
   249→    # Create .erk/impl-context/ with planned-PR provider
   250→    user_output("Creating .erk/impl-context/ folder...")
   251→    create_impl_context(
   252→        plan_content=plan.body,
   253→        plan_id=str(plan_number),
   254→        url=validated.url,
   255→        repo_root=repo.root,
   256→        provider="github-draft-pr",
   257→        objective_id=plan.objective_id,
   258→        now_iso=ctx.time.now().isoformat(),
   259→        node_ids=None,
   260→    )
   261→
   262→    # Stage, commit, and push
   263→    ctx.git.commit.stage_files(repo.root, [IMPL_CONTEXT_DIR], force=True)
   264→    ctx.git.commit.commit(repo.root, f"Add plan for PR #{plan_number}")
   265→    push_result = ctx.git.remote.push_to_remote(
   266→        repo.root, "origin", branch_name, set_upstream=False, force=False
   267→    )
   268→    if isinstance(push_result, PushError):
   269→        raise UserFacingCliError(push_result.message)
   270→    user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + " Branch pushed to remote")
   271→
   272→    # Fix Graphite tracking divergence caused by pull_rebase + commit
   273→    if ctx.graphite_branch_ops is not None:
   274→        ctx.graphite_branch_ops.retrack_branch(repo.root, branch_name)
   275→
   276→    # Switch back to original branch
   277→    ctx.branch_manager.checkout_branch(repo.root, original_branch)
   278→
   279→    # Gather submission metadata
   280→    queued_at = datetime.now(UTC).isoformat()
   281→
   282→    # Load workflow-specific config
   283→    workflow_config = load_workflow_config(repo.root, DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME)
   284→
   285→    # Build inputs dict with plan_backend="planned_pr"
   286→    user_output("")
   287→    user_output(f"Triggering workflow: {click.style(DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME, fg='cyan')}")
   288→
   289→    inputs = {
   290→        "plan_id": str(plan_number),
   291→        "submitted_by": submitted_by,
   292→        "plan_title": validated.title,
   293→        "branch_name": branch_name,
   294→        "pr_number": str(plan_number),
   295→        "base_branch": base_branch,
   296→        "plan_backend": "planned_pr",
   297→        **workflow_config,
   298→    }
   299→
   300→    run_id = ctx.github.trigger_workflow(
   301→        repo_root=repo.root,
   302→        workflow=DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME,
   303→        inputs=inputs,
   304→        ref=None,
   305→    )
   306→    user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + " Workflow triggered.")
   307→
   308→    # Compute workflow URL
   309→    workflow_url = _build_workflow_run_url(validated.url, run_id)
   310→
   311→    # Write dispatch metadata FIRST (before any PR body modification)
   312→    try:
   313→        write_dispatch_metadata(
   314→            plan_backend=ctx.plan_backend,
   315→            github=ctx.github,
   316→            repo_root=repo.root,
   317→            plan_number=plan_number,
   318→            run_id=run_id,
   319→            dispatched_at=queued_at,
   320→        )
   321→        user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + " Dispatch metadata written")
   322→    except Exception as e:
   323→        user_output(
   324→            click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") + f"Failed to update dispatch metadata: {e}"
   325→        )
   326→
   327→    # Update PR body with workflow run link (best-effort)
   328→    # Guard: only append if body is non-empty to avoid overwriting metadata block
   329→    try:
   330→        pr_details = ctx.github.get_pr(repo.root, plan_number)
   331→        if not isinstance(pr_details, PRNotFound) and pr_details.body:
   332→            updated_body = pr_details.body + f"\n\n**Workflow run:** {workflow_url}"
   333→            ctx.github.update_pr_body(repo.root, plan_number, updated_body)
   334→    except Exception as e:
   335→        logger.warning("Failed to update PR body with workflow run link: %s", e)
   336→
   337→    # Post queued event comment via PlanBackend
   338→    try:
   339→        validation_results = {
   340→            "pr_is_open": True,
   341→            "has_erk_plan_label": True,
   342→        }
   343→
   344→        metadata_block = create_submission_queued_block(
   345→            queued_at=queued_at,
   346→            submitted_by=submitted_by,
   347→            plan_number=plan_number,
   348→            validation_results=validation_results,
   349→            expected_workflow=DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_METADATA_NAME,
   350→        )
   351→
   352→        comment_body = render_erk_issue_event(
   353→            title="Plan Queued for Implementation",
   354→            metadata=metadata_block,
   355→            description=(
   356→                f"Plan submitted by **{submitted_by}** at {queued_at}.\n\n"
   357→                f"The `{DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_METADATA_NAME}` workflow has been "
   358→                f"triggered via direct dispatch.\n\n"
   359→                f"**Workflow run:** {workflow_url}"
   360→            ),
   361→        )
   362→
   363→        user_output("Posting queued event comment...")
   364→        ctx.plan_backend.add_comment(repo.root, str(plan_number), comment_body)
   365→        user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + " Queued event comment posted")
   366→    except Exception as e:
   367→        user_output(
   368→            click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow")
   369→            + f"Failed to post queued comment: {e}\n"
   370→            + "Workflow is already running."
   371→        )
   372→
   373→    pr_url = _build_pr_url(validated.url, plan_number)
   374→
   375→    return DispatchResult(
   376→        plan_number=plan_number,
   377→        plan_title=validated.title,
   378→        plan_url=validated.url,
   379→        pr_number=plan_number,
   380→        pr_url=pr_url,
   381→        workflow_run_id=run_id,
   382→        workflow_url=workflow_url,
   383→    )
   384→
   385→
   386→def _detect_plan_number_from_context(
   387→    repo: RepoContext,
   388→    *,
   389→    branch_name: str | None,
   390→) -> int | None:
   391→    """Detect plan PR number from local context when no argument given.
   392→
   393→    Uses resolve_impl_dir() for unified discovery across .erk/impl-context/ directories.
   394→
   395→    Args:
   396→        repo: Repository context
   397→        branch_name: Current git branch name, or None
   398→
   399→    Returns:
   400→        Detected PR number, or None if nothing found.
   401→    """
   402→    impl_dir = resolve_impl_dir(repo.root, branch_name=branch_name)
   403→    if impl_dir is not None:
   404→        plan_ref = read_plan_ref(impl_dir)
   405→        if plan_ref is not None and plan_ref.plan_id.isdigit():
   406→            return int(plan_ref.plan_id)
   407→
   408→    return None
   409→
   410→
   411→@click.command("dispatch")
   412→@click.argument("plan_numbers", type=int, nargs=-1, required=False)
   413→@click.option(
   414→    "--base",
   415→    type=str,
   416→    default=None,
   417→    help="Base branch for PR (defaults to current branch).",
   418→)
   419→@click.pass_obj
   420→def pr_dispatch(ctx: ErkContext, plan_numbers: tuple[int, ...], base: str | None) -> None:
   421→    """Dispatch plans for remote AI implementation via GitHub Actions.
   422→
   423→    Creates branch and draft PR locally (for correct commit attribution),
   424→    then triggers the plan-implement.yml GitHub Actions workflow.
   425→
   426→    Arguments:
   427→        PLAN_NUMBERS: One or more plan numbers to dispatch.
   428→            If omitted, auto-detects from the resolved implementation directory or current branch.
   429→
   430→    \b
   431→    Example:
   432→        erk pr dispatch 123
   433→        erk pr dispatch 123 456 789
   434→        erk pr dispatch 123 --base master
   435→        erk pr dispatch                     # auto-detect from context
   436→
   437→    Requires:
   438→        - All issues must have erk-plan label
   439→        - All issues must be OPEN
   440→        - Working directory must be clean (no uncommitted changes)
   441→    """
   442→    # Validate GitHub CLI prerequisites upfront (LBYL)
   443→    user_output("Checking GitHub authentication...")
   444→    Ensure.gh_authenticated(ctx)
   445→
   446→    # Get repository context
   447→    if isinstance(ctx.repo, RepoContext):
   448→        repo = ctx.repo
   449→    else:
   450→        repo = discover_repo_context(ctx, ctx.cwd)
   451→
   452→    # Ensure trunk is synced before any operations
   453→    user_output("Syncing trunk with remote...")
   454→    ensure_trunk_synced(ctx, repo)
   455→
   456→    # Save current state (needed for both default base and restoration)
   457→    original_branch = ctx.git.branch.get_current_branch(repo.root)
   458→    if original_branch is None:
   459→        user_output(
   460→            click.style("Error: ", fg="red")
   461→            + "Not on a branch (detached HEAD state). Cannot dispatch from here."
   462→        )
   463→        raise SystemExit(1)
   464→
   465→    # If no arguments given, try to auto-detect from context
   466→    if not plan_numbers:
   467→        detected = _detect_plan_number_from_context(repo, branch_name=original_branch)
   468→        if detected is None:
   469→            user_output(
   470→                click.style("Error: ", fg="red")
   471→                + "No plan numbers provided and could not auto-detect from context.\n\n"
   472→                "Provide plan numbers explicitly: erk pr dispatch <number>\n"
   473→                "Or run from a plan branch with an associated PR."
   474→            )
   475→            raise SystemExit(1)
   476→        user_output(f"Auto-detected PR #{detected} from context")
   477→        plan_numbers = (detected,)
   478→
   479→    # Validate base branch if provided, otherwise default to current branch (LBYL)
   480→    if base is not None:
   481→        if not ctx.git.branch.branch_exists_on_remote(repo.root, "origin", base):
   482→            user_output(
   483→                click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"Base branch '{base}' does not exist on remote"
   484→            )
   485→            raise SystemExit(1)
   486→        target_branch = base
   487→    else:
   488→        # If on a placeholder branch (local-only), use trunk as base
   489→        if is_placeholder_branch(original_branch):
   490→            target_branch = ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo.root)
   491→        elif not ctx.git.branch.branch_exists_on_remote(repo.root, "origin", original_branch):
   492→            # Current branch not pushed to remote - fall back to trunk
   493→            target_branch = ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo.root)
   494→        else:
   495→            target_branch = original_branch
   496→
   497→    # Get GitHub username (authentication already validated)
   498→    user_output("Resolving GitHub username...")
   499→    _, username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
   500→    submitted_by = username or "unknown"
   501→
   502→    # Validate all planned-PR plans upfront
   503→    user_output(f"Validating {len(plan_numbers)} planned-PR plan(s)...")
   504→    user_output("")
   505→
   506→    validated_planned_prs: list[ValidatedPlannedPR] = []
   507→    for plan_number in plan_numbers:
   508→        user_output(f"Validating PR #{plan_number}...")
   509→        validated_pr = _validate_planned_pr_for_dispatch(ctx, repo, plan_number)
   510→        validated_planned_prs.append(validated_pr)
   511→
   512→    user_output("")
   513→    user_output(
   514→        click.style("✓", fg="green") + f" All {len(validated_planned_prs)} plan(s) validated"
   515→    )
   516→    user_output("")
   517→
   518→    for v in validated_planned_prs:
   519→        user_output(f"  #{v.number}: {click.style(v.title, fg='yellow')}")
   520→    user_output("")
   521→
   522→    # Dispatch all validated plans
   523→    results: list[DispatchResult] = []
   524→    for i, v in enumerate(validated_planned_prs):
   525→        if len(validated_planned_prs) > 1:
   526→            count = len(validated_planned_prs)
   527→            user_output(f"--- Dispatching PR {i + 1}/{count}: #{v.number} ---")
   528→        else:
   529→            user_output(f"Dispatching PR #{v.number}...")
   530→        user_output("")
   531→        result = _dispatch_planned_pr_plan(
   532→            ctx,
   533→            repo=repo,
   534→            validated=v,
   535→            submitted_by=submitted_by,
   536→            original_branch=original_branch,
   537→            base_branch=target_branch,
   538→        )
   539→        results.append(result)
   540→        user_output("")
   541→
   542→    # Success output
   543→    user_output("")
   544→    user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + f" {len(results)} plan(s) dispatched successfully!")
   545→    user_output("")
   546→    user_output("Dispatched plans:")
   547→    for r in results:
   548→        user_output(f"  #{r.plan_number}: {r.plan_title}")
   549→        user_output(f"    Plan: {r.plan_url}")
   550→        if r.pr_url:
   551→            user_output(f"    PR: {r.pr_url}")
   552→        user_output(f"    Workflow: {r.workflow_url}")
   553→

file.content:
"""Dispatch plans for remote AI implementation via GitHub Actions."""

import logging
import tomllib
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import UTC, datetime
from pathlib import Path

import click

from erk.cli.commands.pr.dispatch_helpers import ensure_trunk_synced
from erk.cli.commands.pr.metadata_helpers import write_dispatch_metadata
from erk.cli.commands.slot.common import is_placeholder_branch
from erk.cli.constants import (
    DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_METADATA_NAME,
    DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME,
    ERK_PLAN_LABEL,
)
from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context
from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure, UserFacingCliError
from erk.core.context import ErkContext
from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext
from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PullRebaseError, PushError
from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import (
    create_submission_queued_block,
    render_erk_issue_event,
)
from erk_shared.gateway.github.parsing import (
    construct_pr_url,
    construct_workflow_run_url,
    extract_owner_repo_from_github_url,
)
from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRNotFound
from erk_shared.impl_context import (
    create_impl_context,
    impl_context_exists,
    remove_impl_context,
)
from erk_shared.impl_folder import read_plan_ref, resolve_impl_dir
from erk_shared.output.output import user_output
from erk_shared.plan_store.planned_pr_lifecycle import IMPL_CONTEXT_DIR
from erk_shared.plan_store.types import PlanNotFound

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def load_workflow_config(repo_root: Path, workflow_name: str) -> dict[str, str]:
    """Load workflow config from .erk/config.toml [workflows.<name>] section.

    Args:
        repo_root: Repository root path
        workflow_name: Workflow filename (with or without .yml/.yaml extension).
            Only the basename is used for config lookup.

    Returns:
        Dict of string key-value pairs for workflow inputs.
        Returns empty dict if config file or section doesn't exist.

    Example:
        For workflow_name="plan-implement.yml", reads from:
        .erk/config.toml -> [workflows.plan-implement] section
    """
    config_path = repo_root / ".erk" / "config.toml"

    if not config_path.exists():
        return {}

    with open(config_path, "rb") as f:
        data = tomllib.load(f)

    # Extract basename and strip .yml/.yaml extension
    basename = Path(workflow_name).name
    config_name = basename.removesuffix(".yml").removesuffix(".yaml")

    # Get [workflows.<name>] section
    workflows_section = data.get("workflows", {})
    workflow_config = workflows_section.get(config_name, {})

    # Convert all values to strings (workflow inputs are always strings)
    return {k: str(v) for k, v in workflow_config.items()}


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class DispatchResult:
    """Result of dispatching a single plan."""

    plan_number: int
    plan_title: str
    plan_url: str
    pr_number: int | None
    pr_url: str | None
    workflow_run_id: str
    workflow_url: str


def _build_workflow_run_url(plan_url: str, run_id: str) -> str:
    """Construct GitHub Actions workflow run URL from plan URL and run ID.

    Args:
        plan_url: GitHub plan URL (e.g., https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123)
        run_id: Workflow run ID

    Returns:
        Workflow run URL (e.g., https://github.com/owner/repo/actions/runs/1234567890)
    """
    owner_repo = extract_owner_repo_from_github_url(plan_url)
    if owner_repo is not None:
        owner, repo = owner_repo
        return construct_workflow_run_url(owner, repo, run_id)
    return f"https://github.com/actions/runs/{run_id}"


def _build_pr_url(plan_url: str, pr_number: int) -> str:
    """Construct GitHub PR URL from plan URL and PR number.

    Args:
        plan_url: GitHub plan URL (e.g., https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123)
        pr_number: PR number

    Returns:
        PR URL (e.g., https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/456)
    """
    owner_repo = extract_owner_repo_from_github_url(plan_url)
    if owner_repo is not None:
        owner, repo = owner_repo
        return construct_pr_url(owner, repo, pr_number)
    return f"https://github.com/pull/{pr_number}"


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ValidatedPlannedPR:
    """Draft PR that passed all validation checks."""

    number: int
    title: str
    url: str
    branch_name: str


def _validate_planned_pr_for_dispatch(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    repo: RepoContext,
    plan_number: int,
) -> ValidatedPlannedPR:
    """Validate a planned PR plan for dispatch.

    Fetches the PR, validates it has the erk-plan label and is OPEN.

    Args:
        ctx: ErkContext with git operations
        repo: Repository context
        plan_number: PR number to validate

    Raises:
        SystemExit: If PR doesn't exist, missing label, or not OPEN.
    """
    pr_result = ctx.github.get_pr(repo.root, plan_number)
    if isinstance(pr_result, PRNotFound):
        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"PR #{plan_number} not found")
        raise SystemExit(1)

    # Validate: must have erk-plan label
    if ERK_PLAN_LABEL not in pr_result.labels:
        user_output(
            click.style("Error: ", fg="red")
            + f"PR #{plan_number} does not have {ERK_PLAN_LABEL} label\n\n"
            "Cannot dispatch non-plan PRs for automated implementation."
        )
        raise SystemExit(1)

    # Validate: must be OPEN
    if pr_result.state != "OPEN":
        user_output(
            click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"PR #{plan_number} is {pr_result.state}\n\n"
            "Cannot dispatch closed PRs for automated implementation."
        )
        raise SystemExit(1)

    return ValidatedPlannedPR(
        number=plan_number,
        title=pr_result.title,
        url=pr_result.url,
        branch_name=pr_result.head_ref_name,
    )


def _dispatch_planned_pr_plan(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    repo: RepoContext,
    validated: ValidatedPlannedPR,
    submitted_by: str,
    original_branch: str,
    base_branch: str,
) -> DispatchResult:
    """Dispatch a validated planned-PR plan for implementation.

    For planned-PR plans, the branch and PR already exist. This function:
    - Fetches and checks out the existing plan branch
    - Creates .erk/impl-context/ with provider="github-draft-pr"
    - Commits and pushes .erk/impl-context/ to existing branch
    - Triggers the workflow with plan_backend="planned_pr"

    Args:
        ctx: ErkContext with git operations
        repo: Repository context
        validated: Validated planned PR information
        submitted_by: GitHub username of submitter
        original_branch: Original branch name (to restore after)
        base_branch: Base branch for PR

    Returns:
        DispatchResult with URLs and identifiers.
    """
    plan_number = validated.number
    branch_name = validated.branch_name

    # Fetch plan content via PlanBackend
    user_output("Fetching plan content...")
    result = ctx.plan_store.get_plan(repo.root, str(plan_number))
    if isinstance(result, PlanNotFound):
        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"PR #{plan_number}: plan content not found")
        raise SystemExit(1)
    plan = result

    # Fetch and checkout the existing plan branch
    user_output(f"Checking out existing plan branch: {click.style(branch_name, fg='cyan')}")
    ctx.git.remote.fetch_branch(repo.root, "origin", branch_name)

    local_branches = ctx.git.branch.list_local_branches(repo.root)
    if branch_name not in local_branches:
        remote_ref = f"origin/{branch_name}"
        ctx.branch_manager.create_tracking_branch(repo.root, branch_name, remote_ref)

    ctx.branch_manager.checkout_branch(repo.root, branch_name)

    # Sync local branch with remote (may be behind from prior submission or CI)
    pull_result = ctx.git.remote.pull_rebase(repo.root, "origin", branch_name)
    if isinstance(pull_result, PullRebaseError):
        raise UserFacingCliError(
            f"Failed to sync branch '{branch_name}' with remote: {pull_result.message}"
        )

    # Clean up previous .erk/impl-context/ if it exists (e.g., from a prior failed dispatch)
    if impl_context_exists(repo.root):
        user_output("Cleaning up previous .erk/impl-context/ folder...")
        remove_impl_context(repo.root)

    # Create .erk/impl-context/ with planned-PR provider
    user_output("Creating .erk/impl-context/ folder...")
    create_impl_context(
        plan_content=plan.body,
        plan_id=str(plan_number),
        url=validated.url,
        repo_root=repo.root,
        provider="github-draft-pr",
        objective_id=plan.objective_id,
        now_iso=ctx.time.now().isoformat(),
        node_ids=None,
    )

    # Stage, commit, and push
    ctx.git.commit.stage_files(repo.root, [IMPL_CONTEXT_DIR], force=True)
    ctx.git.commit.commit(repo.root, f"Add plan for PR #{plan_number}")
    push_result = ctx.git.remote.push_to_remote(
        repo.root, "origin", branch_name, set_upstream=False, force=False
    )
    if isinstance(push_result, PushError):
        raise UserFacingCliError(push_result.message)
    user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + " Branch pushed to remote")

    # Fix Graphite tracking divergence caused by pull_rebase + commit
    if ctx.graphite_branch_ops is not None:
        ctx.graphite_branch_ops.retrack_branch(repo.root, branch_name)

    # Switch back to original branch
    ctx.branch_manager.checkout_branch(repo.root, original_branch)

    # Gather submission metadata
    queued_at = datetime.now(UTC).isoformat()

    # Load workflow-specific config
    workflow_config = load_workflow_config(repo.root, DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME)

    # Build inputs dict with plan_backend="planned_pr"
    user_output("")
    user_output(f"Triggering workflow: {click.style(DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME, fg='cyan')}")

    inputs = {
        "plan_id": str(plan_number),
        "submitted_by": submitted_by,
        "plan_title": validated.title,
        "branch_name": branch_name,
        "pr_number": str(plan_number),
        "base_branch": base_branch,
        "plan_backend": "planned_pr",
        **workflow_config,
    }

    run_id = ctx.github.trigger_workflow(
        repo_root=repo.root,
        workflow=DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME,
        inputs=inputs,
        ref=None,
    )
    user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + " Workflow triggered.")

    # Compute workflow URL
    workflow_url = _build_workflow_run_url(validated.url, run_id)

    # Write dispatch metadata FIRST (before any PR body modification)
    try:
        write_dispatch_metadata(
            plan_backend=ctx.plan_backend,
            github=ctx.github,
            repo_root=repo.root,
            plan_number=plan_number,
            run_id=run_id,
            dispatched_at=queued_at,
        )
        user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + " Dispatch metadata written")
    except Exception as e:
        user_output(
            click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") + f"Failed to update dispatch metadata: {e}"
        )

    # Update PR body with workflow run link (best-effort)
    # Guard: only append if body is non-empty to avoid overwriting metadata block
    try:
        pr_details = ctx.github.get_pr(repo.root, plan_number)
        if not isinstance(pr_details, PRNotFound) and pr_details.body:
            updated_body = pr_details.body + f"\n\n**Workflow run:** {workflow_url}"
            ctx.github.update_pr_body(repo.root, plan_number, updated_body)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning("Failed to update PR body with workflow run link: %s", e)

    # Post queued event comment via PlanBackend
    try:
        validation_results = {
            "pr_is_open": True,
            "has_erk_plan_label": True,
        }

        metadata_block = create_submission_queued_block(
            queued_at=queued_at,
            submitted_by=submitted_by,
            plan_number=plan_number,
            validation_results=validation_results,
            expected_workflow=DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_METADATA_NAME,
        )

        comment_body = render_erk_issue_event(
            title="Plan Queued for Implementation",
            metadata=metadata_block,
            description=(
                f"Plan submitted by **{submitted_by}** at {queued_at}.\n\n"
                f"The `{DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_METADATA_NAME}` workflow has been "
                f"triggered via direct dispatch.\n\n"
                f"**Workflow run:** {workflow_url}"
            ),
        )

        user_output("Posting queued event comment...")
        ctx.plan_backend.add_comment(repo.root, str(plan_number), comment_body)
        user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + " Queued event comment posted")
    except Exception as e:
        user_output(
            click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow")
            + f"Failed to post queued comment: {e}\n"
            + "Workflow is already running."
        )

    pr_url = _build_pr_url(validated.url, plan_number)

    return DispatchResult(
        plan_number=plan_number,
        plan_title=validated.title,
        plan_url=validated.url,
        pr_number=plan_number,
        pr_url=pr_url,
        workflow_run_id=run_id,
        workflow_url=workflow_url,
    )


def _detect_plan_number_from_context(
    repo: RepoContext,
    *,
    branch_name: str | None,
) -> int | None:
    """Detect plan PR number from local context when no argument given.

    Uses resolve_impl_dir() for unified discovery across .erk/impl-context/ directories.

    Args:
        repo: Repository context
        branch_name: Current git branch name, or None

    Returns:
        Detected PR number, or None if nothing found.
    """
    impl_dir = resolve_impl_dir(repo.root, branch_name=branch_name)
    if impl_dir is not None:
        plan_ref = read_plan_ref(impl_dir)
        if plan_ref is not None and plan_ref.plan_id.isdigit():
            return int(plan_ref.plan_id)

    return None


@click.command("dispatch")
@click.argument("plan_numbers", type=int, nargs=-1, required=False)
@click.option(
    "--base",
    type=str,
    default=None,
    help="Base branch for PR (defaults to current branch).",
)
@click.pass_obj
def pr_dispatch(ctx: ErkContext, plan_numbers: tuple[int, ...], base: str | None) -> None:
    """Dispatch plans for remote AI implementation via GitHub Actions.

    Creates branch and draft PR locally (for correct commit attribution),
    then triggers the plan-implement.yml GitHub Actions workflow.

    Arguments:
        PLAN_NUMBERS: One or more plan numbers to dispatch.
            If omitted, auto-detects from the resolved implementation directory or current branch.

    \b
    Example:
        erk pr dispatch 123
        erk pr dispatch 123 456 789
        erk pr dispatch 123 --base master
        erk pr dispatch                     # auto-detect from context

    Requires:
        - All issues must have erk-plan label
        - All issues must be OPEN
        - Working directory must be clean (no uncommitted changes)
    """
    # Validate GitHub CLI prerequisites upfront (LBYL)
    user_output("Checking GitHub authentication...")
    Ensure.gh_authenticated(ctx)

    # Get repository context
    if isinstance(ctx.repo, RepoContext):
        repo = ctx.repo
    else:
        repo = discover_repo_context(ctx, ctx.cwd)

    # Ensure trunk is synced before any operations
    user_output("Syncing trunk with remote...")
    ensure_trunk_synced(ctx, repo)

    # Save current state (needed for both default base and restoration)
    original_branch = ctx.git.branch.get_current_branch(repo.root)
    if original_branch is None:
        user_output(
            click.style("Error: ", fg="red")
            + "Not on a branch (detached HEAD state). Cannot dispatch from here."
        )
        raise SystemExit(1)

    # If no arguments given, try to auto-detect from context
    if not plan_numbers:
        detected = _detect_plan_number_from_context(repo, branch_name=original_branch)
        if detected is None:
            user_output(
                click.style("Error: ", fg="red")
                + "No plan numbers provided and could not auto-detect from context.\n\n"
                "Provide plan numbers explicitly: erk pr dispatch <number>\n"
                "Or run from a plan branch with an associated PR."
            )
            raise SystemExit(1)
        user_output(f"Auto-detected PR #{detected} from context")
        plan_numbers = (detected,)

    # Validate base branch if provided, otherwise default to current branch (LBYL)
    if base is not None:
        if not ctx.git.branch.branch_exists_on_remote(repo.root, "origin", base):
            user_output(
                click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"Base branch '{base}' does not exist on remote"
            )
            raise SystemExit(1)
        target_branch = base
    else:
        # If on a placeholder branch (local-only), use trunk as base
        if is_placeholder_branch(original_branch):
            target_branch = ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo.root)
        elif not ctx.git.branch.branch_exists_on_remote(repo.root, "origin", original_branch):
            # Current branch not pushed to remote - fall back to trunk
            target_branch = ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo.root)
        else:
            target_branch = original_branch

    # Get GitHub username (authentication already validated)
    user_output("Resolving GitHub username...")
    _, username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
    submitted_by = username or "unknown"

    # Validate all planned-PR plans upfront
    user_output(f"Validating {len(plan_numbers)} planned-PR plan(s)...")
    user_output("")

    validated_planned_prs: list[ValidatedPlannedPR] = []
    for plan_number in plan_numbers:
        user_output(f"Validating PR #{plan_number}...")
        validated_pr = _validate_planned_pr_for_dispatch(ctx, repo, plan_number)
        validated_planned_prs.append(validated_pr)

    user_output("")
    user_output(
        click.style("✓", fg="green") + f" All {len(validated_planned_prs)} plan(s) validated"
    )
    user_output("")

    for v in validated_planned_prs:
        user_output(f"  #{v.number}: {click.style(v.title, fg='yellow')}")
    user_output("")

    # Dispatch all validated plans
    results: list[DispatchResult] = []
    for i, v in enumerate(validated_planned_prs):
        if len(validated_planned_prs) > 1:
            count = len(validated_planned_prs)
            user_output(f"--- Dispatching PR {i + 1}/{count}: #{v.number} ---")
        else:
            user_output(f"Dispatching PR #{v.number}...")
        user_output("")
        result = _dispatch_planned_pr_plan(
            ctx,
            repo=repo,
            validated=v,
            submitted_by=submitted_by,
            original_branch=original_branch,
            base_branch=target_branch,
        )
        results.append(result)
        user_output("")

    # Success output
    user_output("")
    user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + f" {len(results)} plan(s) dispatched successfully!")
    user_output("")
    user_output("Dispatched plans:")
    for r in results:
        user_output(f"  #{r.plan_number}: {r.plan_title}")
        user_output(f"    Plan: {r.plan_url}")
        if r.pr_url:
            user_output(f"    PR: {r.pr_url}")
        user_output(f"    Workflow: {r.workflow_url}")

1→"""Shared dispatch logic for one-shot autonomous execution.
     2→
     3→Extracts the branch-create/push/PR/workflow-trigger sequence into reusable
     4→pieces so both `erk one-shot` and `erk objective plan --one-shot` can
     5→dispatch tasks through the same CI workflow.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import logging
     9→import time
    10→from dataclasses import dataclass
    11→from datetime import UTC
    12→
    13→import click
    14→
    15→from erk.cli.commands.pr.metadata_helpers import write_dispatch_metadata
    16→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure
    17→from erk.core.branch_slug_generator import generate_slug_or_fallback
    18→from erk.core.context import ErkContext, NoRepoSentinel, RepoContext
    19→from erk_shared.core.prompt_executor import PromptExecutor
    20→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PushError
    21→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import (
    22→    create_submission_queued_block,
    23→    render_erk_issue_event,
    24→)
    25→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.plan_header import format_plan_header_body
    26→from erk_shared.gateway.github.parsing import construct_workflow_run_url
    27→from erk_shared.gateway.github.pr_footer import build_pr_body_footer
    28→from erk_shared.gateway.time.abc import Time
    29→from erk_shared.naming import (
    30→    format_branch_timestamp_suffix,
    31→    generate_planned_pr_branch_name,
    32→    sanitize_worktree_name,
    33→)
    34→from erk_shared.output.output import format_duration, user_output
    35→from erk_shared.plan_store.planned_pr_lifecycle import build_plan_stage_body
    36→
    37→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    38→
    39→ONE_SHOT_WORKFLOW = "one-shot.yml"
    40→
    41→
    42→@dataclass(frozen=True)
    43→class OneShotDispatchParams:
    44→    """Parameters for dispatching a one-shot workflow."""
    45→
    46→    prompt: str
    47→    model: str | None
    48→    extra_workflow_inputs: dict[str, str]
    49→    slug: str | None
    50→
    51→
    52→@dataclass(frozen=True)
    53→class OneShotDispatchResult:
    54→    """Result of a successful one-shot dispatch."""
    55→
    56→    pr_number: int
    57→    run_id: str
    58→    branch_name: str
    59→
    60→
    61→def generate_branch_name(
    62→    prompt: str,
    63→    *,
    64→    time: Time,
    65→    prompt_executor: PromptExecutor | None,
    66→    slug: str | None,
    67→) -> str:
    68→    """Generate a branch name from the prompt.
    69→
    70→    Format: oneshot-{slug}-{MM-DD-HHMM}
    71→
    72→    When slug is provided, uses it directly (no LLM call). When
    73→    prompt_executor is provided, uses LLM to generate a concise slug
    74→    from the prompt. When None (e.g., dry-run mode), falls back to
    75→    sanitize_worktree_name.
    76→
    77→    Args:
    78→        prompt: The task description
    79→        time: Time gateway for deterministic timestamps
    80→        prompt_executor: PromptExecutor for LLM slug generation, or None to skip
    81→        slug: Pre-generated slug to use directly, or None to generate one
    82→
    83→    Returns:
    84→        Branch name string
    85→    """
    86→    if slug is not None:
    87→        title = slug
    88→    elif prompt_executor is not None:
    89→        title = generate_slug_or_fallback(prompt_executor, prompt)
    90→    else:
    91→        title = prompt
    92→
    93→    sanitized = sanitize_worktree_name(title)
    94→    prefix = "oneshot-"
    95→    max_slug_len = 31 - len(prefix)
    96→    if len(sanitized) > max_slug_len:
    97→        sanitized = sanitized[:max_slug_len].rstrip("-")
    98→    timestamp = format_branch_timestamp_suffix(time.now())
    99→    return f"{prefix}{sanitized}{timestamp}"
   100→
   101→
   102→def dispatch_one_shot(
   103→    ctx: ErkContext,
   104→    *,
   105→    params: OneShotDispatchParams,
   106→    dry_run: bool,
   107→) -> OneShotDispatchResult | None:
   108→    """Execute the full dispatch sequence for a one-shot workflow.
   109→
   110→    Creates branch, commits prompt file directly to branch (no checkout),
   111→    pushes, creates draft PR, and triggers workflow. In dry-run mode,
   112→    prints what would happen without executing.
   113→
   114→    Args:
   115→        ctx: Erk context with git/github gateways
   116→        params: Dispatch parameters
   117→        dry_run: If True, print preview without executing
   118→
   119→    Returns:
   120→        OneShotDispatchResult with pr_number, run_id, branch_name,
   121→        or None in dry-run mode
   122→    """
   123→    # Validate we're in a git repo
   124→    Ensure.invariant(
   125→        not isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel),
   126→        "Not in a git repository",
   127→    )
   128→    assert not isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel)
   129→    repo: RepoContext = ctx.repo
   130→
   131→    # Validate GitHub authentication
   132→    current_step = "Validating GitHub authentication"
   133→    user_output("Validating GitHub authentication...")
   134→    Ensure.gh_authenticated(ctx)
   135→
   136→    # Get GitHub username
   137→    _, username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
   138→    submitted_by = username or "unknown"
   139→    user_output(click.style(f"  \u2713 Authenticated as {submitted_by}", dim=True))
   140→
   141→    # Detect trunk branch
   142→    trunk = ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo.root)
   143→
   144→    # Build PR title
   145→    max_title_len = 60
   146→    suffix = "..." if len(params.prompt) > max_title_len else ""
   147→    pr_title = f"One-shot: {params.prompt[:max_title_len]}{suffix}"
   148→
   149→    if dry_run:
   150→        # In dry-run, show preview without executing
   151→        branch_name = generate_branch_name(
   152→            params.prompt,
   153→            time=ctx.time,
   154→            prompt_executor=None,
   155→            slug=params.slug,
   156→        )
   157→        user_output(
   158→            click.style("Dry-run mode:", fg="cyan", bold=True) + " No changes will be made\n"
   159→        )
   160→        user_output(f"Prompt: {params.prompt}")
   161→        user_output(f"Branch: {branch_name}")
   162→        user_output(f"PR title: {pr_title}")
   163→        user_output(f"Base branch: {trunk}")
   164→        user_output(f"Submitted by: {submitted_by}")
   165→        if params.model is not None:
   166→            user_output(f"Model: {params.model}")
   167→        user_output(f"Workflow: {ONE_SHOT_WORKFLOW}")
   168→        if params.extra_workflow_inputs:
   169→            for key, value in params.extra_workflow_inputs.items():
   170→                user_output(f"Extra input: {key}={value}")
   171→        return None
   172→
   173→    # --- Summary block ---
   174→    user_output(click.style("Dispatching one-shot...", bold=True))
   175→    prompt_preview = params.prompt[:80] + ("..." if len(params.prompt) > 80 else "")
   176→    user_output(click.style(f"  Prompt: {prompt_preview}", dim=True))
   177→    if params.model is not None:
   178→        user_output(click.style(f"  Model: {params.model}", dim=True))
   179→    backend_name = ctx.plan_backend.get_provider_name()
   180→    user_output(click.style(f"  Backend: {backend_name}", dim=True))
   181→    user_output("")
   182→
   183→    start_monotonic = time.monotonic()
   184→
   185→    try:
   186→        objective_issue_str = params.extra_workflow_inputs.get("objective_issue")
   187→        objective_id = int(objective_issue_str) if objective_issue_str else None
   188→
   189→        plan_number: int | None = None
   190→
   191→        # Generate branch name with LLM-generated slug
   192→        current_step = "Generating branch name"
   193→        user_output("Generating branch name...")
   194→        if params.slug is not None:
   195→            slug = params.slug
   196→            user_output(click.style(f"  \u2713 Slug: {slug} (pre-generated)", dim=True))
   197→        else:
   198→            user_output(click.style("  (calling haiku for slug generation...)", dim=True))
   199→            slug_start = time.monotonic()
   200→            slug = generate_slug_or_fallback(ctx.prompt_executor, params.prompt)
   201→            slug_elapsed = time.monotonic() - slug_start
   202→            slug_msg = f"  \u2713 Slug: {slug} ({format_duration(slug_elapsed)})"
   203→            user_output(click.style(slug_msg, dim=True))
   204→        # planned_pr: plnd/ prefix (no issue number needed)
   205→        branch_name = generate_planned_pr_branch_name(
   206→            slug,
   207→            ctx.time.now(),
   208→            objective_id=objective_id,
   209→        )
   210→        user_output(click.style(f"  \u2192 Branch: {branch_name}", dim=True))
   211→
   212→        # Guard against detached HEAD state
   213→        current_branch = ctx.git.branch.get_current_branch(repo.root)
   214→        if current_branch is None:
   215→            user_output(
   216→                click.style("Error: ", fg="red")
   217→                + "Not on a branch (detached HEAD state). Cannot submit from here."
   218→            )
   219→            raise SystemExit(1)
   220→
   221→        # Create branch from trunk
   222→        current_step = "Creating branch"
   223→        user_output("Creating branch...")
   224→        ctx.git.branch.create_branch(repo.root, branch_name, trunk, force=False)
   225→        user_output(click.style("  \u2713 Branch created", dim=True))
   226→
   227→        # Write prompt to .erk/impl-context/prompt.md directly on the branch (no checkout)
   228→        current_step = "Committing prompt file"
   229→        user_output("Committing prompt file...")
   230→        ctx.git.commit.commit_files_to_branch(
   231→            repo.root,
   232→            branch=branch_name,
   233→            files={".erk/impl-context/prompt.md": params.prompt + "\n"},
   234→            message=f"One-shot: {params.prompt[:60]}",
   235→        )
   236→        user_output(click.style("  \u2713 Committed", dim=True))
   237→
   238→        # Push to remote
   239→        current_step = "Pushing to remote"
   240→        user_output("Pushing to remote...")
   241→        push_start = time.monotonic()
   242→        push_result = ctx.git.remote.push_to_remote(
   243→            repo.root, "origin", branch_name, set_upstream=True, force=False
   244→        )
   245→        if isinstance(push_result, PushError):
   246→            Ensure.invariant(False, f"Failed to push branch: {push_result.message}")
   247→        push_elapsed = time.monotonic() - push_start
   248→        user_output(click.style(f"  \u2713 Pushed ({format_duration(push_elapsed)})", dim=True))
   249→
   250→        # --- Create draft PR ---
   251→        current_step = "Creating draft PR"
   252→        user_output("Creating draft PR...")
   253→        # planned_pr: create PR with plan-header metadata block
   254→        _, username_for_header, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
   255→        created_at = ctx.time.now().replace(tzinfo=UTC).isoformat()
   256→        metadata_body = format_plan_header_body(
   257→            created_at=created_at,
   258→            created_by=username_for_header or "unknown",
   259→            worktree_name=None,
   260→            branch_name=branch_name,
   261→            plan_comment_id=None,
   262→            last_dispatched_run_id=None,
   263→            last_dispatched_node_id=None,
   264→            last_dispatched_at=None,
   265→            last_local_impl_at=None,
   266→            last_local_impl_event=None,
   267→            last_local_impl_session=None,
   268→            last_local_impl_user=None,
   269→            last_remote_impl_at=None,
   270→            last_remote_impl_run_id=None,
   271→            last_remote_impl_session_id=None,
   272→            source_repo=None,
   273→            objective_issue=objective_id,
   274→            created_from_session=None,
   275→            created_from_workflow_run_url=None,
   276→            last_learn_session=None,
   277→            last_learn_at=None,
   278→            learn_status=None,
   279→            learn_plan_issue=None,
   280→            learn_plan_pr=None,
   281→            learned_from_issue=None,
   282→            lifecycle_stage="prompted",
   283→        )
   284→        placeholder_content = (
   285→            f"_One-shot: plan content will be populated by one-shot workflow._\n\n"
   286→            f"**Prompt:** {params.prompt}"
   287→        )
   288→        pr_body_initial = build_plan_stage_body(metadata_body, placeholder_content)
   289→        pr_number = ctx.github.create_pr(
   290→            repo.root,
   291→            branch_name,
   292→            pr_title,
   293→            pr_body_initial,
   294→            trunk,

file.content:
"""Shared dispatch logic for one-shot autonomous execution.

Extracts the branch-create/push/PR/workflow-trigger sequence into reusable
pieces so both `erk one-shot` and `erk objective plan --one-shot` can
dispatch tasks through the same CI workflow.
"""

import logging
import time
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import UTC

import click

from erk.cli.commands.pr.metadata_helpers import write_dispatch_metadata
from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure
from erk.core.branch_slug_generator import generate_slug_or_fallback
from erk.core.context import ErkContext, NoRepoSentinel, RepoContext
from erk_shared.core.prompt_executor import PromptExecutor
from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PushError
from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import (
    create_submission_queued_block,
    render_erk_issue_event,
)
from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.plan_header import format_plan_header_body
from erk_shared.gateway.github.parsing import construct_workflow_run_url
from erk_shared.gateway.github.pr_footer import build_pr_body_footer
from erk_shared.gateway.time.abc import Time
from erk_shared.naming import (
    format_branch_timestamp_suffix,
    generate_planned_pr_branch_name,
    sanitize_worktree_name,
)
from erk_shared.output.output import format_duration, user_output
from erk_shared.plan_store.planned_pr_lifecycle import build_plan_stage_body

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

ONE_SHOT_WORKFLOW = "one-shot.yml"


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class OneShotDispatchParams:
    """Parameters for dispatching a one-shot workflow."""

    prompt: str
    model: str | None
    extra_workflow_inputs: dict[str, str]
    slug: str | None


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class OneShotDispatchResult:
    """Result of a successful one-shot dispatch."""

    pr_number: int
    run_id: str
    branch_name: str


def generate_branch_name(
    prompt: str,
    *,
    time: Time,
    prompt_executor: PromptExecutor | None,
    slug: str | None,
) -> str:
    """Generate a branch name from the prompt.

    Format: oneshot-{slug}-{MM-DD-HHMM}

    When slug is provided, uses it directly (no LLM call). When
    prompt_executor is provided, uses LLM to generate a concise slug
    from the prompt. When None (e.g., dry-run mode), falls back to
    sanitize_worktree_name.

    Args:
        prompt: The task description
        time: Time gateway for deterministic timestamps
        prompt_executor: PromptExecutor for LLM slug generation, or None to skip
        slug: Pre-generated slug to use directly, or None to generate one

    Returns:
        Branch name string
    """
    if slug is not None:
        title = slug
    elif prompt_executor is not None:
        title = generate_slug_or_fallback(prompt_executor, prompt)
    else:
        title = prompt

    sanitized = sanitize_worktree_name(title)
    prefix = "oneshot-"
    max_slug_len = 31 - len(prefix)
    if len(sanitized) > max_slug_len:
        sanitized = sanitized[:max_slug_len].rstrip("-")
    timestamp = format_branch_timestamp_suffix(time.now())
    return f"{prefix}{sanitized}{timestamp}"


def dispatch_one_shot(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    params: OneShotDispatchParams,
    dry_run: bool,
) -> OneShotDispatchResult | None:
    """Execute the full dispatch sequence for a one-shot workflow.

    Creates branch, commits prompt file directly to branch (no checkout),
    pushes, creates draft PR, and triggers workflow. In dry-run mode,
    prints what would happen without executing.

    Args:
        ctx: Erk context with git/github gateways
        params: Dispatch parameters
        dry_run: If True, print preview without executing

    Returns:
        OneShotDispatchResult with pr_number, run_id, branch_name,
        or None in dry-run mode
    """
    # Validate we're in a git repo
    Ensure.invariant(
        not isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel),
        "Not in a git repository",
    )
    assert not isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel)
    repo: RepoContext = ctx.repo

    # Validate GitHub authentication
    current_step = "Validating GitHub authentication"
    user_output("Validating GitHub authentication...")
    Ensure.gh_authenticated(ctx)

    # Get GitHub username
    _, username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
    submitted_by = username or "unknown"
    user_output(click.style(f"  \u2713 Authenticated as {submitted_by}", dim=True))

    # Detect trunk branch
    trunk = ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo.root)

    # Build PR title
    max_title_len = 60
    suffix = "..." if len(params.prompt) > max_title_len else ""
    pr_title = f"One-shot: {params.prompt[:max_title_len]}{suffix}"

    if dry_run:
        # In dry-run, show preview without executing
        branch_name = generate_branch_name(
            params.prompt,
            time=ctx.time,
            prompt_executor=None,
            slug=params.slug,
        )
        user_output(
            click.style("Dry-run mode:", fg="cyan", bold=True) + " No changes will be made\n"
        )
        user_output(f"Prompt: {params.prompt}")
        user_output(f"Branch: {branch_name}")
        user_output(f"PR title: {pr_title}")
        user_output(f"Base branch: {trunk}")
        user_output(f"Submitted by: {submitted_by}")
        if params.model is not None:
            user_output(f"Model: {params.model}")
        user_output(f"Workflow: {ONE_SHOT_WORKFLOW}")
        if params.extra_workflow_inputs:
            for key, value in params.extra_workflow_inputs.items():
                user_output(f"Extra input: {key}={value}")
        return None

    # --- Summary block ---
    user_output(click.style("Dispatching one-shot...", bold=True))
    prompt_preview = params.prompt[:80] + ("..." if len(params.prompt) > 80 else "")
    user_output(click.style(f"  Prompt: {prompt_preview}", dim=True))
    if params.model is not None:
        user_output(click.style(f"  Model: {params.model}", dim=True))
    backend_name = ctx.plan_backend.get_provider_name()
    user_output(click.style(f"  Backend: {backend_name}", dim=True))
    user_output("")

    start_monotonic = time.monotonic()

    try:
        objective_issue_str = params.extra_workflow_inputs.get("objective_issue")
        objective_id = int(objective_issue_str) if objective_issue_str else None

        plan_number: int | None = None

        # Generate branch name with LLM-generated slug
        current_step = "Generating branch name"
        user_output("Generating branch name...")
        if params.slug is not None:
            slug = params.slug
            user_output(click.style(f"  \u2713 Slug: {slug} (pre-generated)", dim=True))
        else:
            user_output(click.style("  (calling haiku for slug generation...)", dim=True))
            slug_start = time.monotonic()
            slug = generate_slug_or_fallback(ctx.prompt_executor, params.prompt)
            slug_elapsed = time.monotonic() - slug_start
            slug_msg = f"  \u2713 Slug: {slug} ({format_duration(slug_elapsed)})"
            user_output(click.style(slug_msg, dim=True))
        # planned_pr: plnd/ prefix (no issue number needed)
        branch_name = generate_planned_pr_branch_name(
            slug,
            ctx.time.now(),
            objective_id=objective_id,
        )
        user_output(click.style(f"  \u2192 Branch: {branch_name}", dim=True))

        # Guard against detached HEAD state
        current_branch = ctx.git.branch.get_current_branch(repo.root)
        if current_branch is None:
            user_output(
                click.style("Error: ", fg="red")
                + "Not on a branch (detached HEAD state). Cannot submit from here."
            )
            raise SystemExit(1)

        # Create branch from trunk
        current_step = "Creating branch"
        user_output("Creating branch...")
        ctx.git.branch.create_branch(repo.root, branch_name, trunk, force=False)
        user_output(click.style("  \u2713 Branch created", dim=True))

        # Write prompt to .erk/impl-context/prompt.md directly on the branch (no checkout)
        current_step = "Committing prompt file"
        user_output("Committing prompt file...")
        ctx.git.commit.commit_files_to_branch(
            repo.root,
            branch=branch_name,
            files={".erk/impl-context/prompt.md": params.prompt + "\n"},
            message=f"One-shot: {params.prompt[:60]}",
        )
        user_output(click.style("  \u2713 Committed", dim=True))

        # Push to remote
        current_step = "Pushing to remote"
        user_output("Pushing to remote...")
        push_start = time.monotonic()
        push_result = ctx.git.remote.push_to_remote(
            repo.root, "origin", branch_name, set_upstream=True, force=False
        )
        if isinstance(push_result, PushError):
            Ensure.invariant(False, f"Failed to push branch: {push_result.message}")
        push_elapsed = time.monotonic() - push_start
        user_output(click.style(f"  \u2713 Pushed ({format_duration(push_elapsed)})", dim=True))

        # --- Create draft PR ---
        current_step = "Creating draft PR"
        user_output("Creating draft PR...")
        # planned_pr: create PR with plan-header metadata block
        _, username_for_header, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
        created_at = ctx.time.now().replace(tzinfo=UTC).isoformat()
        metadata_body = format_plan_header_body(
            created_at=created_at,
            created_by=username_for_header or "unknown",
            worktree_name=None,
            branch_name=branch_name,
            plan_comment_id=None,
            last_dispatched_run_id=None,
            last_dispatched_node_id=None,
            last_dispatched_at=None,
            last_local_impl_at=None,
            last_local_impl_event=None,
            last_local_impl_session=None,
            last_local_impl_user=None,
            last_remote_impl_at=None,
            last_remote_impl_run_id=None,
            last_remote_impl_session_id=None,
            source_repo=None,
            objective_issue=objective_id,
            created_from_session=None,
            created_from_workflow_run_url=None,
            last_learn_session=None,
            last_learn_at=None,
            learn_status=None,
            learn_plan_issue=None,
            learn_plan_pr=None,
            learned_from_issue=None,
            lifecycle_stage="prompted",
        )
        placeholder_content = (
            f"_One-shot: plan content will be populated by one-shot workflow._\n\n"
            f"**Prompt:** {params.prompt}"
        )
        pr_body_initial = build_plan_stage_body(metadata_body, placeholder_content)
        pr_number = ctx.github.create_pr(
            repo.root,
            branch_name,
            pr_title,
            pr_body_initial,
            trunk,
            draft=True,
        )
        # Add footer now that we have the PR number.
        footer = build_pr_body_footer(pr_number)
        ctx.github.update_pr_body(repo.root, pr_number, pr_body_initial + footer)
        # Add plan labels
        ctx.github.add_label_to_pr(repo.root, pr_number, "erk-pr")
        ctx.github.add_label_to_pr(repo.root, pr_number, "erk-plan")

        # Key: set plan_number = pr_number so downstream code
        # (workflow inputs, metadata writing, comments) targets the PR
        plan_number = pr_number
        user_output(click.style(f"  \u2192 PR #{pr_number}", dim=True))

        # Build workflow inputs
        # Truncate prompt for workflow input (full text committed to branch)
        max_input_len = 500
        truncated_prompt = params.prompt[:max_input_len]
        if len(params.prompt) > max_input_len:
            truncated_prompt += "... (full prompt committed to .erk/impl-context/prompt.md)"

        inputs: dict[str, str] = {
            "prompt": truncated_prompt,
            "branch_name": branch_name,
            "pr_number": str(pr_number),
            "submitted_by": submitted_by,
            "plan_backend": "planned_pr",
        }
        if params.model is not None:
            inputs["model_name"] = params.model
        if plan_number is not None:
            inputs["plan_issue_number"] = str(plan_number)

        # Merge extra workflow inputs
        inputs.update(params.extra_workflow_inputs)

        # Trigger workflow
        current_step = "Triggering one-shot workflow"
        user_output("Triggering one-shot workflow...")
        run_id = ctx.github.trigger_workflow(
            repo_root=repo.root,
            workflow=ONE_SHOT_WORKFLOW,
            inputs=inputs,
            ref=None,
        )
        user_output(click.style(f"  \u2192 Run ID: {run_id}", dim=True))

        # Compute run_url and queued_at for use in PR body update, metadata, and comment
        run_url: str | None = None
        if repo.github is not None:
            run_url = construct_workflow_run_url(repo.github.owner, repo.github.repo, run_id)
        queued_at = ctx.time.now().replace(tzinfo=UTC).isoformat()

        # Write dispatch metadata and post queued comment (best-effort)
        current_step = "Writing dispatch metadata"
        if plan_number is not None:
            # Write dispatch metadata to plan entity (PR)
            try:
                write_dispatch_metadata(
                    plan_backend=ctx.plan_backend,
                    github=ctx.github,
                    repo_root=repo.root,
                    plan_number=plan_number,
                    run_id=run_id,
                    dispatched_at=queued_at,
                )
                user_output(click.style("\u2713", fg="green") + " Dispatch metadata written")
            except Exception as e:
                user_output(
                    click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow")
                    + f"Failed to update dispatch metadata: {e}"
                )

            # Post queued event comment to plan entity
            current_step = "Posting queued event comment"
            try:
                metadata_block = create_submission_queued_block(
                    queued_at=queued_at,
                    submitted_by=submitted_by,
                    plan_number=plan_number,
                    validation_results={"issue_is_open": True, "has_erk_plan_label": True},
                    expected_workflow="one-shot",
                )
                comment_body = render_erk_issue_event(
                    title="\U0001f504 One-Shot Dispatched",
                    metadata=metadata_block,
                    description=(
                        f"One-shot submitted by **{submitted_by}** at {queued_at}.\n\n"
                        f"**Workflow run:** {run_url or 'N/A'}\n\n"
                        f"**Prompt:** {params.prompt}"
                    ),
                )
                ctx.issues.add_comment(repo.root, plan_number, comment_body)
                user_output(click.style("\u2713", fg="green") + " Queued event comment posted")
            except Exception as e:
                user_output(
                    click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") + f"Failed to post queued comment: {e}"
                )

    except SystemExit:
        raise
    except Exception as exc:
        user_output(click.style(f"Failed during: {current_step}", fg="red"))
        raise exc from None

    # Display results
    elapsed = time.monotonic() - start_monotonic
    duration_str = format_duration(elapsed)
    user_output("")
    user_output(click.style(f"Done! ({duration_str})", fg="green", bold=True))
    if repo.github is not None and run_url is not None:
        pr_url = f"https://github.com/{repo.github.owner}/{repo.github.repo}/pull/{pr_number}"
        user_output(f"PR: {click.style(pr_url, fg='cyan')}")
        user_output(f"Run: {click.style(run_url, fg='cyan')}")
    else:
        user_output(f"PR #{pr_number} created, workflow run {run_id} triggered")

    return OneShotDispatchResult(
        pr_number=pr_number,
        run_id=run_id,
        branch_name=branch_name,
    )
